product_name: Lamaze Gardenbug Foot Finder and Wrist Rattle Set
product_description_under_30_words: A Lamaze Gardenbug set with soft ankle socks and Velcro wrist rattles that jingle when legs and arms move during supervised floor play.
ember_verdict: Kicks start making noise on purpose at this age. Soft bug socks stay put so a flail becomes a rattle without needing a precise grab yet. Use on the playmat during nappy changes when feet are already in the air.

product_description_under_30_words: A Playgro Jungle Friends pack with zebra and giraffe wrist rattles, sock-style foot finders and linking teethers for kick and reach rewards.
ember_verdict: If Gardenbug is already in the drawer, this pack still earns a look for extra foot finders and chew links. Kicks that rattle teach cause and effect without needing a grab first. Rotate pieces when one is drying after a wash.

product_name: Playgro Wrist Rattle and Foot Fingers
product_description_under_30_words: A Playgro gift box of cheerful wrist and foot rattles designed to reward arm and leg movement during supervised floor play.
ember_verdict: A brighter alternative to character jungle packs if you want plain cause-and-effect on wrists and feet. Movement makes the sound, which is the whole job at this age. Stay with supervised awake play — never leave rattles on for sleep.

product_description_under_30_words: A Lamaze Flip Flap Dragon soft rattle with a pram clip and pull-flap wings, useful when a kick or bat toward a clipped toy makes something happen.
ember_verdict: True foot-finder socks are scarce on live UK primaries today. Flip Flap is a companion when a clipped toy rewards a kick beside the socks. Prefer Gardenbug first if you only buy one kick-discovery set. Keep it short, stay close, and stop while it still feels friendly.

product_description_under_30_words: The original Sophie La Girafe teether kept beside kick socks so when feet and hands meet the mouth, a soft rubber chew is ready on the mat.
ember_verdict: Kicking discovery often ends with toes or toys in the mouth. Sophie covers that next beat when socks have done their noisy job. Ranked last because dedicated foot finders are the category spine. Keep it short, stay close, and stop while it still feels friendly.
